next_steps: T2: keep sq-fp8-materialize-smoke as the runtime artifact-boundary guard
T2: keep kup6_gate5_down5 only as a direct-logits regression subset; do not promote it as the SQ quality policy because the token-id model-loop prompt bundle fails strict top1 on len4 and case_a

T2: use sq-fp8-format-candidate-matrix-v0.1.json as the current machine-readable SQ format candidate matrix
T2: treat /tmp/ullm-sq-fp8-kup6-gate5-down5-policy-v0.1-artifact as a reproducible boundary-check artifact, not a committed package or throughput artifact
T2: keep strict_top1 as the promotion rule until a text-level guard is implemented and accepted
T2: use top-k overlap, AQ4 top1 rank, logit gap, workload.final_topk_tokens, and workload.final_topk_logits as diagnostic-only fields

T2: keep layer7-k16-up32 and layer3-kup-plus-layer7-k16 as passing probes, and layer3-kup-plus-layer7-up32 as the current failure guard
Planning: treat current prefill/cached-prefix attention speed as sufficient to resume SQ candidate evaluation; defer extra FlashAttention2-like work unless SQ comparison exposes a blocker

model_loop_coverage_reduction.finding: k/up row-block32 fails case_a even when reduced to layer3 only; up layer3 row-block32 and k layer3 row-block16 pass individually, but k/up layer3 row-block16 fails when combined

mixed_scale_probe.finding: k_proj row-block16 plus up_proj row-block32 passes strict top1 for layer3-only coverage, but fails case_a when expanded to layers 3 and 7
mixed_scale_probe.next_required_probe: layer7-only k16/up32 and one-sided layer7 fallback/scale alternatives

layer7_isolation_probe.finding: layer7 k16/up32 alone passes; layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 k16 passes; layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 up32 fails case_a with AQ4 top1 at SQ top8 rank 3
layer7_isolation_probe.next_required_probe: layer7 up_proj row-block16, row-block64, or fallback while keeping layer3 k16/up32 and layer7 k16 fixed

layer7_up_scale_probe.finding: layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 k16 remains strict-top1 stable only when layer7 up_proj falls back; layer7 up_proj row-block16 and row-block64 both fail case_a with AQ4 top1 at SQ top8 rank 2
layer7_up_scale_probe.next_required_probe: add the next family/layer around layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 k16 while keeping layer7 up_proj fallback until a stronger scale/layout or text-level acceptance rule is available

layer7_add_family_probe.finding: layer7 o_proj row-block32 and layer7 gate_proj row-block32 each preserve strict top1 individually, but layer7 down_proj row-block64 fails case_a and o32+gate32 also fails case_a despite both individual probes passing
layer7_add_family_probe.current_boundary: layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 k16, with layer7 up_proj and down_proj fallback; o_proj and gate_proj are individual branch candidates, not a combined promoted subset
layer7_add_family_probe.next_required_probe: either recover o32+gate32 with stronger scale/layout or choose one branch, o32 or gate32, before widening coverage

layer7_ogate_scale_probe.finding: row-block16 on layer7 o_proj, gate_proj, or both does not recover the combined o/gate failure; all variants fail case_a with SQ top1 193706 and AQ4 top1 at SQ top8 rank 2
layer7_ogate_scale_probe.current_boundary: layer7 o_proj and gate_proj are individual branch candidates only; combined o+gate coverage remains a failure guard under the current W8A16 F32 row-block policy
layer7_ogate_scale_probe.next_required_probe: choose one branch, layer7-plus-o32 or layer7-plus-gate32, before widening coverage; revisit o+gate only with stronger scale layout, dtype, or accepted text-level guard

layer7_o32_branch_layer11_probe.finding: on the layer7 o32 branch, layer11 k_proj row-block16 and layer11 o_proj row-block32 both preserve strict top1 for len4, case_a, and case_b; AQ4 top1 rank is 1 in SQ top8 for all three cases

layer7_o32_branch_layer11_probe.next_required_probe: probe layer15 k_proj row-block16, then layer15 o_proj row-block32 if strict top1 holds, on the same layer7 o32 branch

sq_candidate.initial_target: language model 2D projection, embedding, and lm_head weights

t0_t2_status.t1_component_real_batch_jsonl_parser_not: run-external-benchmark.py --parse ullm-component-prefill preserves batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, request/token parallelism, prefill total input tok/s, attention pair/s, and sampled correctness for component prefill rows.
t0_t2_status.t1_full_package_logical_grid_note: AQ4 full-package package-batch-throughput-bench rows are saved for batch=1/4/8. All rows are status=ok and verified_all=true, and preserve prefill/decode/end-to-end total throughput, KV cache bytes, VRAM, and correctness. These rows are logical batch only: batching.mode=logical, prefill_real_batch=false, decode_real_batch=false, runtime_reused_across_requests=false, and weights_reloaded_per_request=true. Real request-batch prefill/decode/end-to-end full-package rows are still required before SQ throughput comparison.
t0_t2_status.t1_jsonl_schema_preservation_note: run-external-benchmark.py preserves package-batch total-throughput metrics, prefix/chunk/context accounting, executor accounting, and memory.kv_cache_bytes_total in inference-benchmark-result-v0.1 JSONL rows.
t0_t2_status.t1_model_loop_hybrid_throughput_note: package-self-attn-mlp-block-model-loop-smoke emits timed prefill/decode/end-to-end token fields plus CSV workload fields; run-external-benchmark.py --parse ullm-model-loop-throughput converts it to inference-benchmark-result-v0.1. The R9700 layers 3,7 smoke is a synthetic selected-layer stack row, not final full LM throughput. It preserves batching.mode=hybrid, prefill_real_batch=false, decode_real_batch=true, and decode_executor_request_parallelism=2.
t0_t2_status.t1_package_prefill_component_runner_note: tools/run-package-prefill-component-workload.py runs package-backed component smoke commands through run-external-benchmark.py --parse ullm-component-prefill; R9700 AQ4 k-proj smoke preserved batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, executor=aq4_matvec_batch_f32, token_parallelism=2, and prefill_total_input_tokens_per_second=19063.596157. The flattened batch grid adds batch_size=4, prompt_tokens_per_request=[2,2,2,2], component_total_input_tokens=8, prefill_executor_token_parallelism=8, prefill_executor_request_parallelism=1, and prefill_total_input_tokens_per_second=65513.626834.
t0_t2_status.t1_token_id_model_loop_bridge_note: package-token-ids-model-loop-smoke feeds embedding token IDs into the selected-layer model-loop scheduler and emits final lm_head top1 tokens. The R9700 layers 3,7 smoke used batch=2, prompt=2, generated=1 and preserved input_source=embedding_token_ids, batching.mode=hybrid, decode_real_batch=true, decode_executor_request_parallelism=2, final_top1_tokens=[155793,23175], and verified=true.
t0_t2_status.t1_token_id_model_loop_real_prefill_brid: package-token-ids-model-loop-smoke now groups prefill by layer/timestep across active requests and emits prefill_batch_request_counts_csv. The R9700 layers 3,7 smoke used batch=2, prompt=2, generated=1 and preserved batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, prefill_executor=stack_prefill_request_batch_step, prefill_batch_request_counts=[2,2,2,2], decode_real_batch=true, decode_executor_request_parallelism=2, final_top1_tokens=[155793,23175], and verified=true.
t0_t2_status.t1_self_attn_stack_real_batch_note: package-token-ids-model-loop-smoke now accepts all-self-attn/manifest-self-attn and derives sorted self-attention layer indices from q_norm/k_norm passthrough tensors in the package manifest. R9700 AQ4 all-self-attn rows for layers 3,7,11,15,19,23,27,31 preserve B=4/B=8 batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, decode_real_batch=true, and verified_all=true. The measured B=1/4/8 end-to-end tok/s values are 73.358179, 73.078709, and 72.851718. This is a manifest self-attention stack row only, not full mixed-attention LM throughput; linear-attention layers and full package real-batch execution remain required.
t0_t2_status.t1_full_mixed_layer_kind_inventory_note: package-layer-kind-inventory-smoke with manifest-all verified the AQ4 full package layer order as contiguous 0..31, with self-attention layers 3,7,11,15,19,23,27,31 and 24 linear-attention layers. This inventory fixes the full mixed-attention runner implementation target, but is not a throughput row. Real request-batch full package prefill/decode/end-to-end rows still require per-request linear-attention recurrent state and Conv1d history.
t0_t2_status.t1_linear_attn_request_state_owner_note: PackageLinearAttnResidentStepBatchLayer maps RequestId to per-request PackageLinearAttnResidentStepLayer slots, keeping linear-attention recurrent state and Conv1d history isolated by request. Unit tests guard empty request-list rejection, duplicate request-id rejection, and stable request order mapping. This is a runner-side state boundary, not a throughput row or shared-weight final design.
t0_t2_status.t1_linear_attn_request_state_smoke_note: package-linear-attn-request-state-smoke ran R9700 AQ4 layer 0 with request_count=2 and sequence_len=2. The interleaved PackageLinearAttnResidentStepBatchLayer output matched per-request serial PackageLinearAttnResidentStepLayer references with serial_reference_max_abs_diff=0, nonfinite_count=0, and unknown_request_rejected=true. This verifies package execution for the request-state owner, but it is not a throughput row or the final shared-weight design.
t0_t2_status.t1_self_attn_request_state_owner_note: PackageSelfAttnResidentStepBatchLayer maps RequestId to per-request PackageSelfAttnResidentStepLayer slots, keeping self-attention paged KV cache, written_len, and block table isolated by request. Unit tests guard empty request-list rejection, duplicate request-id rejection, and stable request order mapping for self-attn and linear-attn request slot helpers. This aligns self-attn and linear-attn request-id dispatch for the next full mixed layer enum, but is not a throughput row or shared-weight final design.
t0_t2_status.t1_mixed_request_state_layer_enum_smoke_: package-token-ids-mixed-request-state-smoke ran R9700 AQ4 layers 0,3 with batch=2, prompt=2, and generated=1. It connected token ID embeddings, PackageLinearAttnResidentStepBatchLayer, PackageSelfAttnResidentStepBatchLayer, final RMSNorm, and lm_head top1 guard with verified=true and final_top1_tokens=[151353,151353]. This is batching.mode=request_state_interleaved, not a throughput row, not shared-weight final design, and still has prefill_real_batch=false and decode_real_batch=false.
t0_t2_status.t2_artifact_metadata_path_note: build-sq-fp8-w8a16-artifact.py accepts --policy-json for sq-fp8-policy-v0.1; dry-run confirmed kup6_gate5_down5 resolves to 22 FP8 tensors, 753 passthrough tensors, and row_block32 scale. A real payload artifact was generated under /tmp with compact_resident_bytes_estimate=18579553248 and materialized_working_set_bytes_estimate=201326592. The builder now also supports policy scale.overrides[] for per-tensor scale layout probes; mixed artifacts keep per-entry scale metadata authoritative.
t0_t2_status.t2_candidate_matrix_note: tools/build-sq-fp8-candidate-matrix.py builds a machine-readable candidate matrix from the current kup6_gate5_down5 policy and policy artifact result. It fixes W8A16 F32-scale, scale16, scale8, W8A8, and hybrid fallback candidate axes; strict top1 remains the promotion rule and overlay load timing remains excluded from speed results.
t0_t2_status.t2_runtime_load_smoke: sq-fp8-materialize-smoke cpu0/r9700 fixture path and r9700 policy artifact tensor path verified
t0_t2_status.t2_short_prompt_guard_note: One selected q_proj tensor and then the layer 3 projection set are loaded from SQ FP8 while the rest falls back to AQ4. The layer 3 projection-set guard matched AQ4 top1 in 3/3 short token-ID cases. Expanding to layers 3,7 changed top1 for attention-only, MLP-only, and attention+MLP overlays, while AQ4 top1 remained inside SQ top8. Family split shows q/v/down are risky for strict top1, while k/o/gate/up preserve top1 individually and as a combined subset across 3/3 short prompts. Expanding k/o/gate/up to layers 3,7,11,15 preserved top1 in 2/3 short prompts and failed case_a. Row-block scale recovers q at block32 and down at block64, but v remains strict-top1 risky for tested block sizes. A mixed candidate with v fallback and q/k/o/gate/up/down row-block32 FP8 passes layers 3,7,11,15 in 3/3 short prompts and layers 3,7,11,15,19 on len4. The same policy fails layers 3,7,11,15,19,23 and all self-attention probe layers. Layer 23 alone is fixed by q/v fallback, but 6-layer q/v fallback still fails, so cumulative drift remains. T2 promotion rule v0.1 is strict_top1; top-k/rank/gap are diagnostic-only. Six-layer family split shows k/up row-block32 passes strict top1 and k/up combined passes 3/3 short prompts, but o/gate/down fail individually at 6 layers. Per-layer combination search shows k/up all 6 plus any one or two of o/gate/down on layers 3,7,11,15,19 pass len4, while adding all three fails len4. The kup6_gate5_down5 prompt bundle passes len4, case_a, and case_b strict top1; case_a top8 overlap is only 2/8, so it is a six-layer regression subset rather than a full SQ policy. The selected FP8/fallback policy is saved as sq-fp8-kup6-gate5-down5-policy-v0.1 and was materialized into a real payload artifact for runtime boundary verification. Full-target SQ guard is still pending and needs broader coverage, stronger format decisions, or a formally accepted text-level guard. A stricter six-layer token-id model-loop prompt-bundle guard later showed that the same kup6_gate5_down5 artifact fails strict top1 on len4 and case_a when token-id embedding, selected runtime layers, request-batch prefill, decode ready batch, final norm, and lm_head are connected in one path; AQ4 top1 remains in SQ top8. Therefore the direct logits prompt-bundle pass is weaker evidence than the model-loop guard. Coverage reduction on the stricter model-loop gate then showed k/up row-block32 still fails case_a even when reduced to layer3 only; up_proj layer3 row-block32 and k_proj layer3 row-block16 pass individually, but k/up layer3 row-block16 fails when combined. Therefore the next T2 step is mixed per-family/per-tensor scale-layout support rather than widening k/up coverage. Mixed scale support then showed k row-block16 plus up row-block32 recovers layer3-only coverage but fails case_a when expanded to layers 3 and 7, so layer7 coverage interaction is now the immediate T2 boundary. Layer7 isolation then showed layer7 k16/up32 alone and layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 k16 pass, while layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 up32 fails case_a. The immediate next probe should target layer7 up_proj scale or fallback. Layer7 up scale probing then showed that keeping layer7 up_proj as fallback preserves strict top1 for layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 k16, while layer7 up_proj row-block16 and row-block64 both fail case_a. Therefore the current T2 model-loop-safe boundary keeps layer7 up_proj in fallback. Layer7 add-family probing then showed that layer7 o_proj row-block32 and gate_proj row-block32 pass individually, while layer7 down_proj row-block64 and the o32+gate32 combination fail case_a. Therefore o/gate are branch candidates, not a combined promoted subset under strict_top1. Layer7 o/gate scale probing then showed that o16+gate32, o32+gate16, and o16+gate16 all fail case_a, so row-block16 does not recover combined o/gate coverage. Layer7 o32 branch expansion then showed that adding layer11 k_proj row-block16, and then layer11 o_proj row-block32, both preserve strict top1 across len4, case_a, and case_b. Therefore the current model-loop-safe branch is layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 k16/o32 plus layer11 k16/o32, with layer7 up/down still fallback; the next probe is layer15 k_proj row-block16 on the same branch. Layer15 o32 branch expansion then showed that adding layer15 k_proj row-block16, and then layer15 o_proj row-block32, both preserve strict top1 across len4, case_a, and case_b. Therefore the current model-loop-safe branch is layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 k16/o32 plus layer11 k16/o32 plus layer15 k16/o32, with layer7 up/down still fallback; the next probe is layer19 k_proj row-block16 on the same branch. Layer19 o32 branch expansion then showed that adding layer19 k_proj row-block16, and then layer19 o_proj row-block32, both preserve strict top1 across len4, case_a, and case_b. Therefore the current model-loop-safe branch is layer3 k16/up32 plus layer7 k16/o32 plus layer11 k16/o32 plus layer15 k16/o32 plus layer19 k16/o32, with layer7 up/down still fallback; the next probe is layer23 k_proj row-block16 on the same branch. Layer23 o32 branch expansion then showed that adding layer23 k_proj row-block16, and then layer23 o_proj row-block32, both preserve strict top1 across len4, case_a, and case_b. Therefore the current model-loop-safe branch is layer3 k16/up32 plus layers 7/11/15/19/23 k16/o32, with layer7 up/down still fallback; the next probe is layer3 o_proj row-block32 to close the selected-layer k/o coverage gap. Layer3 o32 branch expansion then showed that adding layer3 o_proj row-block32 to the selected-layer k/o branch preserves strict top1 across len4, case_a, and case_b. Therefore the current model-loop-safe branch is layer3 k16/o32/up32 plus layers 7/11/15/19/23 k16/o32, with layer7 up/down/gate fallback; the next probe is layer3 gate_proj row-block32 on the same branch. Layer3 gate scale probing then showed that adding layer3 gate_proj row-block32 changes len4 top1 from 110784 to 102446; row-block16 does not recover this failure. Therefore the current model-loop-safe branch remains selected-layer-ko-plus-layer3-o32, layer3 gate_proj row-block32/16 are failure guards, and the next probe is layer3 down_proj row-block64 on the same branch. Layer3 down64 probing then showed that adding layer3 down_proj row-block64 preserves strict top1 across len4, case_a, and case_b. Therefore the current model-loop-safe branch is selected-layer-ko-plus-layer3-o32-down64, layer3 gate_proj row-block32/16 remain failure guards, and the next probe is layer11 up_proj row-block32 on the same branch. Layer11 up scale probing then showed that adding layer11 up_proj row-block32 changes len4 top1 from 110784 to 102446; row-block16 does not recover this failure. Therefore the current model-loop-safe branch remains selected-layer-ko-plus-layer3-o32-down64, layer11 up_proj row-block32/16 are failure guards, and the next probe is layer11 down_proj row-block64 on the same branch. Layer11 down64 probing then showed that adding layer11 down_proj row-block64 preserves strict top1 across len4, case_a, and case_b. Therefore the current model-loop-safe branch is selected-layer-ko-layer3-down64-plus-layer11-down64, layer11 up_proj row-block32/16 remain failure guards, and the next probe is layer15 down_proj row-block64 on the same branch.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_token_id_model_loop_bridge_note: sq-fp8-token-ids-model-loop-smoke connects the current kup6_gate5_down5 SQ FP8 artifact to the token-id model-loop request-batch prefill path. The R9700 layers 3,7 smoke used batch=2, prompt=2, generated=1 and preserved batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, decode_real_batch=true, sq_overlay=true, final_top1_tokens=[155793,23175], and verified=true. This is a selected-layer bridge, not final full LM throughput.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_token_id_model_loop_prompt_bundle_: sq-fp8-token-ids-model-loop-smoke was run on the current kup6_gate5_down5 SQ FP8 artifact with layers 3,7,11,15,19,23, batch=3, top_k=8, and len4/case_a/case_b prompt bundle. Both AQ4 and SQ rows preserved batching.mode=real, prefill_real_batch=true, decode_real_batch=true, workload.final_topk_tokens, and workload.final_topk_logits. SQ matched AQ4 top1 only on case_b; len4 and case_a drifted while AQ4 top1 remained inside SQ top8 at ranks 3 and 2. Internal SQ tok/s was close to AQ4 on this selected-layer path, but wrapper elapsed includes artifact read/materialization. Under the current strict_top1 promotion rule, kup6_gate5_down5 remains a regression subset and is not promoted as the SQ quality policy.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_model_loop_coverage_reduction_note: R9700 token-id model-loop prompt bundle coverage reduction showed that k/up row-block32 still fails case_a from six layers down to layer3-only coverage. up_proj layer3 row-block32 and k_proj layer3 row-block16 pass strict top1 as single-tensor probes, but combined k/up layer3 row-block16 fails case_a. This blocks direct promotion and makes per-family/per-tensor scale-layout support the next T2 requirement.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_mixed_scale_layout_note: build-sq-fp8-w8a16-artifact.py supports policy scale.overrides[] and emits candidate scale_granularity=mixed with scale_layout=per_tensor. R9700 model-loop prompt bundle shows kup1-layer3-k16-up32 passes 3/3, but kup2-k16-up32 fails case_a with AQ4 top1 at SQ top8 rank 2. This makes layer7 isolation the next T2 quality step.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_layer7_isolation_note: R9700 model-loop prompt bundle shows layer7-k16-up32 passes 3/3 and layer3-kup-plus-layer7-k16 passes 3/3. layer3-kup-plus-layer7-up32 fails case_a with AQ4 top1 at SQ top8 rank 3. This isolates the immediate failure boundary to layer7 up_proj interaction with the layer3 passing mixed-scale probe.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_layer7_up_scale_note: R9700 model-loop prompt bundle shows layer7-up-fallback passes 3/3, while layer7-up16 and layer7-up64 both fail case_a. AQ4 top1 remains in SQ top8 at rank 2 for the failing variants. Current passing boundary is layer3 k_proj row-block16, layer3 up_proj row-block32, and layer7 k_proj row-block16 with layer7 up_proj fallback.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_layer7_add_family_note: R9700 model-loop prompt bundle shows layer7-plus-o32 and layer7-plus-gate32 pass 3/3. layer7-plus-down64 fails case_a with AQ4 top1 at SQ top8 rank 2. layer7-plus-o32-gate32 also fails case_a with AQ4 top1 at SQ top8 rank 2, so o/gate are individual branch candidates rather than a combined promoted subset.
t0_t2_status.t2_sq_layer7_ogate_scale_note: R9700 model-loop prompt bundle shows o16+gate32, o32+gate16, and o16+gate16 all fail case_a with SQ top1 193706 and AQ4 top1 at SQ top8 rank 2. Row-block16 is not enough to recover combined o/gate coverage.
